title: Leveraging qPCR for Real-Time Monitoring of Halal Integrity
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Ensuring   halal   integrity     across     complex     food     supply     chains   has   become     a     pressing     challenge  ,   especially     with     the     rise     of     processed     and     imported     products  . Real-  time   PCR (  qPCR  )   provides     a     rapid  ,   sensitive  ,   and     specific     method     to     detect   non-halal   ingredients     at     the   DNA level. By   enabling   real-  time     monitoring  ,   qPCR     allows     for     immediate     detection     of     contamination     or     fraud  ,   reducing     the     risk     of   halal   breaches  .   This     molecular     approach     is     critical     for     building     consumer     trust  ,   protecting   halal   certification     credibility  ,   and     supporting     regulatory     compliance   in   both     domestic     and     international     markets  .   Integrating     qPCR     into     routine     surveillance     programs     enhances     the     capacity     of   halal   assurance     systems  ,   safeguarding     religious     requirements     and     preventing     economic     losses     due     to     recalls     or     certification     disputes  .
